summary: Tight, upbeat set with standout Hard To Handle and China>Rider
blurb: The show stays tight and fun, delivering strong performances of Hard To Handle, Good Lovin', and the China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue. Jerry Garcia shines with a steel‑guitar duet alongside NRPS while the band keeps the energy high throughout both sets.
review: On April 22, 1971 at Bangor Municipal Auditorium the Dead offered a short but power‑cut‑limited set that never lost its momentum. The first set builds nicely to a rousing Casey Jones after tight renditions of Bertha, Next Time You See Me and a punchy Hard To Handle. The second set ramps up intensity with a sparkling China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue, a spirited Greatest Story Ever Told into Beat It On Down The Line, and two takes of Good Lovin' that showcase the band's playful chemistry. Jerry’s steel‑guitar duet with New Riders of the Purple Sage adds an extra sparkle, and his vocal work is described as “nice singing and playing.” Recorded on a pristine soundboard often dubbed the “Super 71’ Soundboard,” this performance captures every nuance without dropout or balance issues, making it the definitive listening source for this classic early‑70s show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis is uniformly positive, praising the tight playing, standout songs (Hard To Handle, China>Rider, Good Lovin'), and excellent soundboard capture. No negative comments were found, supporting a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, strong mix and no dropouts; widely praised as the “Super 71’ Soundboard.”

key_highlights: Tight, upbeat set that stays fun from start to finish.
Jerry Garcia’s steel‑guitar duet with NRPS adds a unique sparkle.
Strong performances of Hard To Handle, Good Lovin', and the China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue.
Pristine soundboard recording captures every nuance.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a superb steel‑guitar duet with NRPS, plus nice singing and playing throughout.
